explanatory_strength.rationale: The packet lays out a capital-cycle deleveraging story (FY2025 OCF $3.1B, mid-cycle owner cash ~$4.70/sh, ~$29B debt) with four additive proof components, but that is not a quality-reinvestment mechanism. Moat is none, dhando is none, archetype is capital_cycle with levered payoff lens, FY2025 net income is $111M, and capex $3.8B exceeded OCF so reinvestable owner earnings are not demonstrated.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Core operating facts reconcile from full-tier FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q (revenue $54.6B, operating income $1.5B, OCF $3.1B, debt ~$29B, ~658.6M diluted shares); all four component calculation proofs are mechanically valid with empty double_counting_flags. Decision-grade gaps for this persona remain: management_facts has zero claims; AAdvantage co-brand base $8/sh is judgment-only; maintenance versus growth capex and reinvestment ROIC are not filing-verified.

downside_control.rationale: Permanent equity loss is plausible and not bounded: ~$28B net debt against $902M cash and $3.7B equity, levered payoff lens, dhando none, and the five-question gate fails bounded bear. Contract low case implies -$41.70/sh component value; simultaneous earnings trough and credit tightening could force dilutive refinancing before deleveraging completes.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Filing-locked component base sums to -$9.79/sh at $15.14, so contract base and low annualized returns are null on negative intrinsic value; high case is only 8.12% over seven years versus the 12% Buffett/Weschler hurdle. Lawrence synthesis 28.48% and bull 35% rely on unproven levered recovery and contradict the conservative proof schedule; no evidenced path clears the owner-earnings reinvestment bar.

claims.claim: American Airlines is outside the Buffett/Weschler quality_reinvestment power zone: capital_cycle archetype, moat none, dhando none, and a levered equity stub on ~$29B debt — not a durable compounder with reinvestable owner earnings at a knowable price.

Proof-first component schedule base case is -$9.79/sh ($33.88 network + $8.00 co-brand - $42.67 net financial claims - $9.00 cycle reserve) versus ~$15.14 price; using FY2025 OCF per share as owner cash overstates distributable earnings when capex $3.8B exceeded OCF and net income was $111M.

At spot, no filing-supported return path clears the 12% owner-earnings hurdle (contract high case 8.12% annualized only); capital deployment is not approvable on quality-reinvestment criteria despite understandable cyclical recovery optionality.

strongest_counter_explanation: Post-pandemic U.S. airline consolidation, premium-cabin mix, and AAdvantage co-brand mile sales have structurally lifted mid-cycle cash generation; steady $3B+ operating cash flow will pay down the $29B debt stack without dilution, making today's ~$15 equity a mispriced option on deleveraging that will re-rate toward $25+ per share as net debt falls and normalized owner cash compounds.
most_important_missing_fact: Filing-supported maintenance versus growth capex split plus a two-year net-debt reduction schedule funded by owner cash without dilutive refinancing — required to test whether the $4.70/sh OCF proxy is truly reinvestable owner earnings rather than cyclical cash consumed by mandatory fleet capital.
falsifiers: Total debt falls below $24B while operating income exceeds $2.5B for two consecutive quarters without equity issuance.
Trailing four-quarter owner cash per share (operating cash flow minus disclosed maintenance capex) exceeds $5.50 with diluted share count stable within 1%.
SEC filings disclose standalone AAdvantage co-brand economics with incremental margin above 40% and major partner contract renewal visibility through 2030.
specialist_findings: Outside power zone (capital_cycle, moat none, dhando none, ls_algo_underlying; Buffett rules require compounder/infrastructure archetype plus stable/widening moat). Audited all four valuation-proof rows: midcycle_passenger_network owner_cash_or_dividend_discount reconciles from $4.7053/sh OCF proxy × 7.2× capitalization to $33.88 base but treats operating cash flow as owner earnings while FY2025 capex $3.779B exceeded OCF $3.099B; aadvantage_co_brand_option risk_adjusted_milestone_value base $8/sh with success_probability null and no standalone filing disclosure; net_financial_claims filing-locked at -$42.67/sh; cycle_leverage_reserve -$9/sh additive for fuel/recession stress. No comparables attached. Overlap controls pass (four unique overlap keys, no double_counting_flags). Option scan: co-brand valued separately; fleet and loyalty embedded_in_segment. Contract base annualized return withheld at negative intrinsic; high case 8.12% at $15.14. Lawrence synthesis 28.48% diverges from filing-locked component sum and is not usable for this persona without unproven deleveraging proof. Historical Berkshire exit (2020) is context tier only.
